symptoms.symptom_name: Complete inability to void for 14 hours with severe suprapubic pain
Progressive LUTS (hesitancy, poor stream, nocturia) over 6 months

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Underlying bladder outlet obstruction from presumed BPH.
Alpha-adrenergic agonist increases urethral sphincter tone, precipitating acute-on-chronic retention.
Absence of fever makes a primarily infective cause less likely, though it does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable, dull, tender suprapubic mass to the umbilicus with bladder volume >1000mL

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms a massively distended bladder; clinician-only finding sealed from telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Complete inability to void with progressive LUTS and a recent decongestant trigger together confirm acute-on-chronic urinary retention requiring urgent catheter decompression.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bedside bladder ultrasound scan and urethral catheterisation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms bladder volume and provides immediate decompression; cannot be performed via telehealth.
Screens for post-renal acute kidney injury from prolonged retention.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Outpatient urology referral as the first step tonight
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Would dangerously delay urgent bladder decompression needed tonight.
